   <item>
      <title>NBA, NBPA Agree To Make Cade Cunningham, Luka Doncic Eligible For Awards</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285139/NBA-NBPA-Agree-To-Make-Cade-Cunningham-Luka-Doncic-Eligible-For-Awards</link>
      <description><p>The NBA and NBPA have agreed, in accordance with the Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA), that Detroit Pistons guard Cade Cunningham and Los Angeles Lakers guard Luka Doncic are eligible for all <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/ballot">annual awards</a> for the 2025-26 NBA regular season. </p>
<p>Cunningham played in 63 qualified games due in part to missing 12 games as a result of a collapsed lung that was diagnosed on March 17.  </p>
<p>Doncic played in 64 qualified games due in part to missing two games to attend the birth of his daughter in Slovenia.  </p>
<p>The NBA and NBPA agreed that, taking into account the totality of the circumstances for Cunningham and Doncic, each player qualified for awards under the extraordinary circumstances provision in the CBA.</p>
<p>Additionally, Minnesota Timberwolves guard Anthony Edwards, who played in 60 qualified games, filed an extraordinary circumstances challenge under the CBA, seeking award eligibility before an independent arbitrator. Edwards' challenge was denied and, as a result, he is not eligible for annual awards for the 2025-26 NBA regular season.  </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 11:51:32 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285139/NBA-NBPA-Agree-To-Make-Cade-Cunningham-Luka-Doncic-Eligible-For-Awards</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Cunningham_Cade_det_250428.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Ja Morant Has Informed Players, Former Coaches He's Done Playing For Grizzlies</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285140/Ja-Morant-Has-Informed-Players-Former-Coaches-Hes-Done-Playing-For-Grizzlies</link>
      <description><p>Ja Morant has privately told players around the league and some of his former coaches that he is no longer willing to play for the Memphis Grizzlies, multiple league sources told ESPN.</p>
<p>The breaking point traces directly to a one-game suspension Morant received early in the season following a locker room confrontation with head coach Tuomas Iisalo after a Halloween night loss to the Los Angeles Lakers. Iisalo had called out Morant, who shot 3-for-14 in 31 minutes, for what the coach deemed a substandard effort. Morant, already bristling over Iisalo's substitution patterns, responded dismissively and condescendingly, sources said.</p>
<p>The organization sided firmly with Iisalo, which was a pointed message that Morant has never processed. Teammates were also instructed to give Morant space and leave him alone during the suspension period, leaving him feeling isolated by the people closest to him.</p>
<p>The estrangement stands in stark contrast to Morant's only public comments on his future, delivered after a 24-point, 13-assist performance against Orlando in London in January.</p>
<p>"I've got a logo on my back, so that should tell you exactly where I want to be," Morant said, referencing a Grizzlies tattoo.</p>
<p>Despite those words, sources indicate Morant's private position tells a different story.</p>
<p>Grizzlies general manager Zach Kleiman offered measured public comments during his exit interview Monday.</p>
<p>"Ja's been a pro," Kleiman said. "I think everyone is on the same page as much as we can be. I won't speculate on any potential transactions."</p>
<p>Morant, 26, did not participate in Monday's exit interviews. He appeared in just 20 games this season, averaging 19.5 points on a career-worst 41.0% shooting before a UCL sprain in his left elbow ended his year on January 21, a night he punctuated with a spectacular chase-down block that may have been his final act in a Grizzlies uniform.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 12:05:49 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285140/Ja-Morant-Has-Informed-Players-Former-Coaches-Hes-Done-Playing-For-Grizzlies</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Morant_Ja_mem_241028.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Darius Garland, Ty Lue Only Members Of Clippers Certain To Return Next Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285148/Darius-Garland-Ty-Lue-Only-Members-Of-Clippers-Certain-To-Return-Next-Season</link>
      <description><p>Tyronn Lue and Darius Garland are the only two confirmed pieces of the Los Angeles Clippers' future following their play-in elimination loss to the Golden State Warriors, as the franchise heads into an offseason clouded by roster uncertainty and an ongoing NBA investigation.</p>
<p>Lue is under contract as head coach through 2029. Garland, acquired at the February trade deadline from the Cleveland Cavaliers in the James Harden deal, is signed through 2028. A league source told The Athletic that both will return next season.</p>
<p>Kawhi Leonard has one year left on his contract, and the franchise must decide whether to extend him or pursue a trade. Another league source said the Clippers believe the NBA's investigation into an alleged salary cap circumvention scheme involving Leonard will not produce significant punishment and that his contract will not be voided.</p>
<p>"I think we're going to be in the clear," Leonard said after Wednesday's loss. "I'm not stressing."</p>
<p>The NBA draft lottery on May 10 looms as a pivotal date. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/detailed">If the Indiana Pacers' pick falls outside the top four</a>, the Clippers would select fifth or sixth, a potential factor in whether Leonard and the club opt to run it back together.</p>
<p>John Collins enters unrestricted free agency. Bennedict Mathurin, a restricted free agent, wants a starting role. Bradley Beal is expected to return from hip surgery but carries a player option. Veteran role players Nicolas Batum, Brook Lopez and Bogdan Bogdanovic all carry team options the Clippers could decline.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Los-Angeles-Clippers/12/playoff-history">The franchise has now gone five straight seasons without a playoff series victory when they reached the Western Conference Finals in 2021</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 22:31:46 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285148/Darius-Garland-Ty-Lue-Only-Members-Of-Clippers-Certain-To-Return-Next-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Garland_Darius_lac_260306.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Wizards Retain Brian Keefe As Head Coach For 2026-27 Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285145/Wizards-Retain-Brian-Keefe-As-Head-Coach-For-2026-27-Season</link>
      <description><p>Washington Wizards general manager Will Dawkins confirmed Thursday that head coach Brian Keefe will return for the 2026-27 season, transitioning the franchise from a deliberate rebuild toward a competitive push led by Trae Young and Anthony Davis.</p>
<p>Keefe compiled a 43-160 record across two-plus rebuilding seasons, including a 17-65 mark this year. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/analysis/285118/Ten-Year-NBA-Net-Rating-Rankings-Infographics-For-All-30-Teams">The Wizards have ranked last in Net Rating each of the past two seasons</a>. After losing 26 of their final 27 games, the Wizards secured the NBA's worst record, guaranteeing no worse than the fifth pick in <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/lottery_simulator">May's draft lottery</a> and preserving <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/detailed">a top-eight protected first-round pick</a> owed to the New York Knicks.</p>
<p>The front office credited Keefe with accelerating the development of Alex Sarr, Kyshawn George, Tre Johnson, Will Riley and Jamir Watkins during a period designed to prioritize youth over victories.</p>
<p>Monumental Basketball president Michael Winger assessed Keefe's performance plainly in January.</p>
<p>"I think Brian has done a remarkable job," Winger said. "He's developed young athletes. He's built a culture of competitiveness, accountability, togetherness and joy. I'm very, very pleased with the job that Brian has done."</p>
<p>Keefe, Dawkins and Winger share deep organizational roots as all three spent formative stretches with the Oklahoma City Thunder, creating a philosophical alignment that has strengthened the coach's standing with team officials.</p>
<p>Players have responded to Keefe's personal investment as much as his basketball acumen. Bub Carrington recalled that during the 2024 Summer League in Las Vegas, when his father was hospitalized with a blood clot, Keefe spent several days at the hospital with the family.</p>
<p>When doctors prevented Carrington's father from flying home to Baltimore due to blood-thinning medication, Keefe volunteered to make the drive back with them.</p>
<p>"I think that's the type of guy B.K. is," Carrington said.</p>
<p>Keefe was elevated from lead assistant to interim head coach midway through the 2023-24 season following Wes Unseld Jr.'s dismissal, and was hired permanently in May 2024 after a formal search that included outside candidates.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 12:45:23 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285145/Wizards-Retain-Brian-Keefe-As-Head-Coach-For-2026-27-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Keefe_Brian_was_240314.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA In Active Talks With Kalshi, Polymarket On Prediction Market Deal</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285146/NBA-In-Active-Talks-With-Kalshi-Polymarket-On-Prediction-Market-Deal</link>
      <description><p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">The NBA is in active discussions with prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket about a potential partnership deal, with talks gaining significant momentum in recent months, multiple sources familiar with the matter told Front Office Sports.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">Informal discussions have been underway for more than a year, but accelerated after Michael Selig, confirmed as chairman of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission in December, began engaging directly with leagues and signaling regulatory intent.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">"It was his engagement that accelerated talks," one source said. "He signaled he has every intention of putting real regulation in place with proper protections."</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">The NBA is focused on maintaining control over which markets are permitted and securing mechanisms to monitor suspicious trading activity, sources said. The CFTC has flagged markets tied to player injuries and unsportsmanlike conduct as particularly vulnerable to manipulation.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">No firm timeline exists for a deal, though multiple sources said an agreement could be reached before next season. Unlike MLB, which signed exclusively with Polymarket, the NBA may partner with both platforms simultaneously, mirroring the NHL's approach.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">The MLB-Polymarket deal, worth up to $300 million over four years according to sources, is viewed as a potential benchmark for future agreements.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">"Everyone sees these dollar signs," one prediction-market industry executive said.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">In a sign of advancing discussions, NBA EVP Scott Kaufman-Ross presented an update on the talks to team presidents this week. Kalshi and Polymarket founders also appeared on the most-attended panel at the NBA's technology summit during All-Star Weekend in February.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">Adam Silver said during that weekend the league was viewing prediction markets similarly to traditional sports betting partners. The NBA currently holds official sports betting partnerships with DraftKings and FanDuel.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">Representatives for the NBA, CFTC, Kalshi and Polymarket declined to comment.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 13:41:53 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285146/NBA-In-Active-Talks-With-Kalshi-Polymarket-On-Prediction-Market-Deal</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Silver_Adam_nba_240331.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Napheesa Collier, Lynx Sign One-Year, $1.4M Supermax</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285149/Napheesa-Collier-Lynx-Sign-One-Year-$14M-Supermax</link>
      <description><p>Napheesa Collier has agreed to sign a one-year, $1.4 million supermax contract with the Minnesota Lynx. Collier joins Kelsey Mitchell and A'ja Wilson on supermax contracts.</p>
<p>Collier was the runner-up in WNBA MVP voting in 2025 for a second consecutive season. Collier averaged 22.9 points and 7.3 rebounds last season.</p>
<p>In January, Unrivaled announced Collier would be sidelined for four to six months after requiring surgery on both her left and right ankles.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 22:42:56 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285149/Napheesa-Collier-Lynx-Sign-One-Year-$14M-Supermax</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/wnba_minnesotalynx.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Donovan Mitchell Hopes To Play With Cavaliers 'For As Long As He Can'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285132/Donovan-Mitchell-Hopes-To-Play-With-Cavaliers-For-As-Long-As-He-Can</link>
      <description><p>Donovan Mitchell is excited about the Cleveland Cavaliers potential heading into the 2026 NBA Playoffs. Mitchell has been enthused about his backcourt partnership with James Harden. He hopes they can fuel a deep postseason run and success beyond this year.</p>
<p>When asked if anything has changed with how he might feel about signing and extension with the Cavs, Mitchell made it clear that he doesn't want to play anywhere else.</p>
<p>"I love Cleveland. I've said it before: I want to play here for as long as I can. And the goal is to win - as long as we're continuing to win at the highest level. But I love it, man. It's a place that I feel like I can call home, you know what I mean?" Mitchell answered.</p>
<p>Earlier in the interview, Mitchell wondered why people don't believe him when he says he likes being in Cleveland. The Cavs star guard said he's enjoyed being in the organization and his place within the team's hierarchy.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 09:40:17 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285132/Donovan-Mitchell-Hopes-To-Play-With-Cavaliers-For-As-Long-As-He-Can</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Mitchell_Donovan_cle_250422.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>

   <item>
      <title>Grizzlies Expected To Have Wider Market For Ja Morant This Summer</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285134/Grizzlies-Expected-To-Have-Wider-Market-For-Ja-Morant-This-Summer</link>
      <description><p>The Memphis Grizzlies are expected to re-engage teams in trade talks involving star point guard Ja Morant this offseason. Executives around the league believe Memphis will find a wider market for Morant this summer, but that the Grizzlies shouldn't expect to get much value in return.</p>
<p>Memphis did well to acquire <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/team">seven first-round picks in trades</a> for Jaren Jackson Jr. and Desmond Bane in the last year. That kind of return isn't likely in a Morant trade. When the Grizzlies engaged the Sacramento Kings in talks ahead of the trade deadline, the Kings wanted Memphis to send assets with Morant. That reportedly quickly ended those trade discussions.</p>
<p>Memphis general manager Zach Kleiman wouldn't go into details about the team's plans for Morant this offseason but hinted that there is an understanding.</p>
<p>"Ja's been a pro," Kleiman said. "I think everyone is on the same page as much as we can be. I won't speculate on any potential transactions."</p>
<p>It may take some other dominoes to fall in the draft and free agency before Morant's market becomes clearer, but it's believed that there will be interest in the former All-Star.</p>
<p>"There will be teams that will feel like they didn't get what they wanted in the draft or free agency, and that's when something could happen for Ja," a Western Conference executive told ESPN.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 09:55:03 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285134/Grizzlies-Expected-To-Have-Wider-Market-For-Ja-Morant-This-Summer</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Morant_Ja_mem_260116.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Kawhi Leonard Defers On Clippers Future As Contract Extension Window Approaches</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285131/Kawhi-Leonard-Defers-On-Clippers-Future-As-Contract-Extension-Window-Approaches</link>
      <description><p>Kawhi Leonard declined to address his future with the Los Angeles Clippers following Wednesday's season-ending play-in loss to Golden State, entering an offseason that carries significant uncertainty both contractually and legally.</p>
<p>"Let me cry about this loss a little bit more," Leonard said when asked whether he could envision remaining in Los Angeles. "We'll have our discussions when that time comes."</p>
<p>Leonard enters the final year of his contract next season and becomes eligible to sign a two-season extension the day after the NBA Finals. The Clippers can offer a maximum of $126.1 million across those two seasons.</p>
<p>The offseason decision arrives against the backdrop of an ongoing NBA investigation into an endorsement deal Leonard signed with Aspiration, a now-bankrupt former team sponsor. Podcaster Pablo Torre reported before the season that former Aspiration employees believed the arrangement was structured to circumvent the salary cap.</p>
<p>Leonard was measured when asked about the investigation's status Wednesday, </p>
<p>"I never thought about it too much other than questions asked. You'll have to ask the NBA, not me. I'm not the one doing the investigation," Leonard said. "I think we're going to be in the clear. I'm not stressing it."</p>
<p>Leonard's on-court performance this season was among the finest of his career. He averaged a career-best 27.9 points per game, appeared in 66 games and ranked in the top 10 in field goal percentage, 2-point percentage and 3-point percentage. Los Angeles outscored opponents by 7.8 points per 100 possessions with him on the floor and was outscored by 6.9 with him off it.</p>
<p>Wednesday's finale, however, exposed his limitations against a focused defensive scheme. Golden State devoted its game plan to neutralizing Leonard, who managed just one made shot over the final 16 minutes and committed five second-half turnovers, his most in any half since 2019.</p>
<p>The Warriors were among teams that contacted the Clippers about Leonard's availability following February's James Harden trade, according to multiple sources. Those discussions are expected to resurface this summer.</p>
<p>The Clippers showed resilience despite the midseason roster overhaul, becoming the first team in NBA history to finish 15 games over .500 after reaching 15 games under at an earlier point. Los Angeles closed the regular season 36-19, fourth best in the Western Conference over that stretch, after opening 6-21.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 07:21:42 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285131/Kawhi-Leonard-Defers-On-Clippers-Future-As-Contract-Extension-Window-Approaches</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Leonard_Kawhi_lac_250423.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Stephen Curry: These Nights Make Everything Worth It</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285130/Stephen-Curry-These-Nights-Make-Everything-Worth-It</link>
      <description><p>Stephen Curry missed 27 consecutive games with a right knee injury where he considered whether to shut down his season entirely, but he returned for four regular-season games for the chance at the Play-In. Curry delivered in the first one with 35 points, 11 in the final 9:27, including a decisive pull-up 3-pointer that broke a 117-117 tie with 50 seconds remaining to lead the Golden State Warriors to a 126-121 victory over the Los Angeles Clippers</p>
<p>"This is what you work all year for, all summer, all offseason," Curry said. "We're not guaranteed a series yet. But these nights make everything worth it."</p>
<p>Curry shot just 2-for-9 in the first half, absorbing contact and finding few clean looks. He grew visibly frustrated with missed calls. </p>
<p>"He can be in the middle of a very average game. And then all of a sudden, it clicks," Steve Kerr said. "He's so poised out there. Having a rough go, he just keeps going and keeps going. All it takes is one. You can feel it."</p>
<p>Curry responded in the second half with 16 third-quarter points, then 11 more in the fourth.</p>
<p>"I always talk about earning and deserving the confidence you play with because of the work you put in," Curry said. "Not being afraid of failure, missed shots, turnovers. You just keep going."</p>
<p>Kerr, who called Wednesday's victory among his favorites in over a decade coaching the franchise, refused to entertain any notion that Curry's return was ill-advised.</p>
<p>"This is why Steph came back. Everybody out there who thought Steph should've taken the rest of the year off," Kerr said, pausing before pounding the podium. "This is what he does. This is who he is. If he can compete, he's going to compete. It was just incredible to watch."</p>
<p>"He's coming in every single day putting the work in and, for a large amount of time, not getting any results. Most people would quit," Draymond Green said. "But that's never who he's been. That's why this organization has been able to go from worst to first. When you have a guy like that, that the only thing that matters is the next day and not the next year, just the next day - everyone else falls in line."</p>
<p>Green added that Curry's composure through adversity sets the standard for everyone around him.</p>
<p>"When the head of the snake is locked in like that and everything is going wrong for him and he stayed dialed in and continuing to push forward, no one else is allowed to drop their head," Green said. "Man, I'm blessed to play with him for 14 years. He's a special, special guy."</p>
<p>Golden State now faces the Phoenix Suns on Friday, with the winner advancing to face the Oklahoma City Thunder. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 07:13:25 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285130/Stephen-Curry-These-Nights-Make-Everything-Worth-It</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Curry_Stephen_gsw_220523.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>Chris Finch Says Timberwolves Have To 'Flip' Switch Versus Nuggets</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285128/Chris-Finch-Says-Timberwolves-Have-To-Flip-Switch-Versus-Nuggets</link>
      <description><p>Head coach Chris Finch said that it's up to the Minnesota Timberwolves to turn on the "switch" ahead of their matchup against the Denver Nuggets in the first round of the playoffs.</p>
<p>"We know the team we can be and who we have been. It's about whether we can maintain that," Finch said. "You don't ever really want to be a flip-the-switch team, but we do have a switch to flip, and we have to flip it now. When we do that, everybody kind of becomes the best version of themselves and that brings out that continuity and connection that we need."</p>
<p>Game 1 of the series starts on Saturday.</p>
<p>The Wolves finished with a 49-33 record to get the sixth seed in the Western Conference.</p>
<p>Anthony Edwards admitted that the team may have struggled this season as they looked forward to the playoffs.</p>
<p>"It felt like that at times, like we were just trying to get through the season to get to the playoffs, but we're here now, and all the other excuses are out the window," added Edwards.</p>
<p>Edwards finished the year averaging a career-high 28.8 points per game on 48.9% shooting. </p>
<p>Edwards also played a career-low 61 games and missed eight out of the last 11 games with a knee injury. </p>
<p>"I haven't played a lot in the last month," Edwards said, "so I'm trying to get back in shape."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 02:13:23 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285128/Chris-Finch-Says-Timberwolves-Have-To-Flip-Switch-Versus-Nuggets</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Finch_Chris_min_221012.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Alex Condon To Return To Florida For Senior Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285127/Alex-Condon-To-Return-To-Florida-For-Senior-Season</link>
      <description><p>Alex Condon announced that he will return to Florida for his senior season on Wednesday.</p>
<p>Condon is currently the No. 30 prospect on ESPN's Top 100.</p>
<p>"When the team succeeds, everyone succeeds off the court too," Condon said about his decision to return. "I feel like it could really be the same for us individually [next season]. I want to be first-team All-SEC and in the talks for All-American. And I think the way that I closed out the season, if I'm able to be that consistent for a whole season, the sky's the limit for [my] draft stock."</p>
<p>Condon averaged 15.1 points and 7.5 rebounds last season.</p>
<p>Condon is projected to be a first-round pick in the 2027 draft. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 01:58:18 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285127/Alex-Condon-To-Return-To-Florida-For-Senior-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_Florida.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Tyrese Maxey Leads Sixers Past Magic To Set Up Celtics First-Round Matchup</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285126/Tyrese-Maxey-Leads-Sixers-Past-Magic-To-Set-Up-Celtics-First-Round-Matchup</link>
      <description><p>Tyrese Maxey scored 31 points and rookie VJ Edgecombe posted a double-double as the Philadelphia 76ers defeated the Orlando Magic 109-97 Wednesday, claiming the Eastern Conference's No. 7 seed and a first-round playoff series against the second-seeded Boston Celtics beginning Sunday at TD Garden.</p>
<p>Maxey logged 42 minutes with six assists while Edgecombe delivered 19 points and 11 rebounds in 41 minutes. The Bahamian rookie received a technical foul for taunting Jalen Suggs following an acrobatic finish but remained a productive force throughout.</p>
<p>"I guess that happens when you let a kid play in such a high-intensity game," Edgecombe said. "But I was out there having fun. So if I got to play wild for us to win, I'll play wild."</p>
<p>Joel Embiid, sidelined after an emergency appendectomy last Thursday in Houston, was present on the bench, his first appearance with the team since the procedure. His status for the Boston series remains undetermined as he continues his recovery.</p>
<p>Nick Nurse acknowledged the path forward won't be straightforward.</p>
<p>"It wasn't pretty. It wasn't easy and wasn't pretty, but we're here now," Nurse said. "And now, we got to see what we can do with it."</p>
<p>Paul George addressed the magnitude of the upcoming challenge directly.</p>
<p>"We're playing against winners," George said. "We're playing against champions. This is where they play their best basketball. They're a well-oiled machine over there. Great coach, great roster, two studs, superstars over there. So it's going to be a challenge. We just got to be ready for it."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285133/How-To-Watch-Magic-vs-Hornets-Play-In-Game-Live-Stream-TV-Channel-Predictions">Orlando, meanwhile, faces the Charlotte Hornets at home Friday</a> in an elimination contest, with the winner advancing to face top-seeded Detroit. The Hornets are <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">slight favorites</a> after the opening odds were set. Paolo Banchero shot 7-for-22 with six turnovers in the defeat.</p>
<p>Philadelphia gets three days off before the Celtics series tips Sunday at 1 p.m. ET.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 01:20:45 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285126/Tyrese-Maxey-Leads-Sixers-Past-Magic-To-Set-Up-Celtics-First-Round-Matchup</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Maxey_Tyrese_phi_231220.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Stephen Curry, Draymond Green Lead Warriors To 126-121 Comeback Win Over Clippers</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285125/Stephen-Curry-Draymond-Green-Lead-Warriors-To-126-121-Comeback-Win-Over-Clippers</link>
      <description><p>Stephen Curry scored 35 points and Draymond Green neutralized Kawhi Leonard down the stretch as the Golden State Warriors rallied from 13 points down to defeat the Los Angeles Clippers 126-121 Wednesday, surviving the NBA play-in tournament's first round.</p>
<p>Curry, who returning last week from a 27-game absence due to a right knee injury, delivered 11 of his game-high total in the final 9:27. Green recorded two critical steals in the final 49 seconds, including a clean dribble strip of Leonard in isolation that sealed the outcome.</p>
<p>"This is why Steph came back," Warriors coach Steve Kerr said. "Everyone out there who thought Steph should take the rest of the year off - this is what he does. This is who he is."</p>
<p>Green held Leonard to 21 points on 8-of-17 shooting. According to Genius IQ, Green defended Leonard across 12 half-court possessions in the fourth quarter without surrendering a single shot attempt, the most such matchups against Leonard in a fourth quarter without allowing a shot by any defender since Leonard joined the Clippers in 2019.</p>
<p>"Draymond, Hall of Fame defender," Leonard said. "It was hard to even get shots up."</p>
<p>Kristaps Porzingis contributed an and-1 floater and a transition 3-pointer to keep Golden State within striking distance. Al Horford, 39, buried four fourth-quarter 3-pointers.</p>
<p>Golden State entered as <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/clippers-warriors-playin-prediction-04-15-2026/">five-point underdogs</a> after going 9-18 without Curry and losing starting wings Jimmy Butler III and Moses Moody to season-ending knee injuries.</p>
<p>"Most people would quit," Green said. "Especially when you're in a position like that."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285135/How-To-Watch-Warriors-Vs-Suns-Play-In-Game-Live-Stream-TV-Channel-Predictions">The Warriors now travel to face the Phoenix Suns on Friday</a> in another elimination contest where <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/">they will be underdogs again</a>. The winner claims the Western Conference's 8-seed and a first-round matchup against defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 01:14:30 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285125/Stephen-Curry-Draymond-Green-Lead-Warriors-To-126-121-Comeback-Win-Over-Clippers</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Curry_Stephen_gsw_191007.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>LaMelo Ball Fined $35,000, Assessed Flagrant Foul 2 On Bam Adebayo Play</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285122/LaMelo-Ball-Fined-$35000-Assessed-Flagrant-Foul-2-On-Bam-Adebayo-Play</link>
      <description><p>LaMelo Ball has been fined $35,000 and assessed a Flagrant Foul 2 upon league office review for making unnecessary and reckless contact with Miami Heat center-forward Bam Adebayo that created a significant injury risk.</p>
<p>The incident occurred with 11:13 remaining in the second quarter of the Charlotte Hornets' 127-126 overtime win over the Heat on April 14 in the NBA Play-In Tournament.</p>
<p>Ball has been fined an additional $25,000 for using profane language during a live postgame television interview.</p>
<p>Ball will not be suspended for Friday's Play-In game. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 22:00:40 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285122/LaMelo-Ball-Fined-$35000-Assessed-Flagrant-Foul-2-On-Bam-Adebayo-Play</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Ball_Lamelo_cha_201215.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Investigating LaMelo Ball For Tripping Bam Adebayo In Play-In</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285121/NBA-Investigating-LaMelo-Ball-For-Tripping-Bam-Adebayo-In-Play-In</link>
      <description><p>LaMelo Ball is under investigation by the NBA for tripping Miami Heat center Bam Adebayo during Tuesday's play-in game, which the Charlotte Hornets won 127-126 in overtime.</p>
<p>The league has contacted both franchises and will interview Ball, sources said. The incident is under review for a possible flagrant foul 1 or flagrant foul 2 upgrade, though sources indicated a suspension is unlikely.</p>
<p>Adebayo suffered a lower back injury on the play and did not return, finishing with just 11 minutes of action. Ball scored the winning layup with 4.7 seconds remaining.</p>
<p>The play occurred early in the second quarter. After Ball's shot was blocked, Adebayo grabbed the rebound near the sideline. Ball, falling to the floor, swiped at Adebayo's foot, sending him hard to the ground with no whistle. Officials could not review the play because it was not stopped immediately.</p>
<p>"The play wasn't whistled in real time," referee Zach Zarba told a pool reporter. "Play continued with a fast break. And because play wasn't stopped immediately, and there was no whistle on the play, the window to review the play was closed."</p>
<p>Erik Spoelstra called for Ball's ejection following the loss.</p>
<p>"I don't think it's cute," Spoelstra said. "I don't think it's funny. I think it's a stupid play. It's a dangerous play. Obviously, our best player was out. I'm not making an excuse. The Hornets played great and they made those plays down the stretch. We had our opportunities to win. That's a shame. You should be penalized for that. I don't think that belongs in the game. Tripping guys, shenanigans."</p>
<p>Ball issued an apology after the game.</p>
<p>"I apologize on that one," Ball said. "I got hit in the head [on the play] and didn't really know where I was. But I'm going to check on him, see if he's OK and everything."</p>
<p>Following the game, video surfaced of a January 2024 regular-season encounter in which Ball appeared to deliberately reach for Adebayo's leg while on the floor.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 19:52:49 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285121/NBA-Investigating-LaMelo-Ball-For-Tripping-Bam-Adebayo-In-Play-In</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Ball_Lamelo_cha_201215.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Regular Season Viewership Hits 24-Year High At 170M U.S. Viewers</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285120/NBA-Regular-Season-Viewership-Hits-24-Year-High-At-170M-US-Viewers</link>
      <description><p>The NBA drew 170 million U.S. viewers during the 2025-26 regular season across its four primary broadcast platforms, which is its strongest viewership figures in 24 years and an 86 percent increase over the prior season.</p>
<p>The numbers mark Year 1 of the league's 11-year, $76 billion-plus media rights agreement signed in 2024, which added Amazon Prime Video and restored NBC/Peacock to the broadcast lineup alongside returning partners ABC/ESPN and NBA TV.</p>
<p>Average viewership across all four platforms reached its highest point in 13 years, climbing 35 percent over last season. A total of 57 telecasts drew average audiences of at least 2 million viewers, the most since 2011-12.</p>
<p>Fans logged more than 920 million total viewing hours, up 25% year-over-year and also the best figure since 2011-12.</p>
<p>The All-Star Game on NBC averaged 8.8 million viewers, the largest midseason showcase audience in 15 years. NBA Cup group stage games posted a 90 percent viewership gain.</p>
<p>Off the court, the league's social media platforms generated a record 228 billion views according to Videocites, a 13% rise over last season.</p>
<p>Arena attendance over the past three seasons has surpassed any comparable three-year span in league history, the NBA added.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 15:50:50 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285120/NBA-Regular-Season-Viewership-Hits-24-Year-High-At-170M-US-Viewers</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Brown_Jaylen_bos_250504.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Sean Sweeney Considered NBA's Leading First-Time Head Coach Candidate</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285119/Sean-Sweeney-Considered-NBAs-Leading-First-Time-Head-Coach-Candidate</link>
      <description><p>Sean Sweeney is expected to be one of the leading head coaching candidates during the upcoming offseason cycle. Sweeney joined the San Antonio Spurs as associate head coach under Mitch Johnson last year after four seasons as the lead assistant for Jason Kidd with the Dallas Mavericks. </p>
<p>"He's the top available (first-time NBA head coach) by a wide margin," one Eastern Conference scout told The Athletic.</p>
<p>Sweeney was brought up to The Athletic the most amongst insiders when inquiring about potential first-time coaches.</p>
<p>Before joining the Mavericks, Sweeney was an assistant coach under Kidd during the early part of Giannis Antetokounmpo's career where he was the future two-time MVP's primary player development coach. </p>
<p>Sweeney then worked under Dwane Casey with the Detroit Pistons. </p>
<p>The Milwaukee Bucks and New Orleans Pelicans are the two teams currently with head coaching vacancies.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 15:45:27 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285119/Sean-Sweeney-Considered-NBAs-Leading-First-Time-Head-Coach-Candidate</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_sanantoniospurs.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>A'ja Wilson, Aces Sign WNBA Record Three-Year, $5M Supermax Contract</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285117/Aja-Wilson-Aces-Sign-WNBA-Record-Three-Year-$5M-Supermax-Contract</link>
      <description><p>A'ja Wilson has agreed to sign a three-year, $5 million supermax contract with the Las Vegas Aces. The deal is the largest in WNBA history and was negotiated by Jade-Li English of Klutch Sports Group.</p>
<p>Wilson is a three-time WNBA champion and four-time WNBA Most Valuable Player.</p>
<p>Wilson was the first overall pick in the 2018 WNBA draft out of South Carolina after also winning a NCAA championship in college.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/player/Aja-Wilson/Summary/3001159">During her MVP season in 2025</a>, Wilson averaged 23.4 points, 10.2 rebounds, 3.0 assists, 2.3 blocks and 1.6 steals with a PER of 33.3. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wnba/stats/2025/Advanced_Stats/Qualified/points/All/desc/1/Regular_Season">Wilson's WNBA leading PER was well ahead of second ranked Napheesa Collier at 29.8</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 14:36:51 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285117/Aja-Wilson-Aces-Sign-WNBA-Record-Three-Year-$5M-Supermax-Contract</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wilson_Aja_lva_240522.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Deni Avdija's 41 Points Leads Blazers Back To Playoffs For First Time Since 2021</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285116/Deni-Avdijas-41-Points-Leads-Blazers-Back-To-Playoffs-For-First-Time-Since-2021</link>
      <description><p>Deni Avdija scored 41 points to go with 12 assists and seven rebounds in the Portland Trail Blazers' 114-110 win at the Phoenix Suns to clinch the No. 7 seed in the Western Conference Playoffs. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Portland-Trail-Blazers/24/playoff-history">The Blazers hadn't reached the playoffs since 2021</a> when they were eliminated in the first round by the Denver Nuggets in six games.</p>
<p>Avdija struggled in the second half of the regular season, but he showcased why he was an All-Star for the first time in his career with a playoff spot on the line.</p>
<p>"It's the best thing that's ever happened to me in my career so far," Avdija said.</p>
<p>Portland's outstanding perimeter defenders limited Devin Booker to just 17 field goal attempts as he finished with 22 points. Jalen Green scored 35 points on 14-for-29 from the floor.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/boxscore/2026-04-14/Portland-at-Phoenix/522371">The Blazers scored 1.194 points per possession compared to 1.152 for the Suns</a>.</p>
<p>The Blazers will play the San Antonio Spurs in the first round as Victor Wembanyama makes his playoff debut. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/nba/playoffs/odds/">The Spurs are heavy favorites in the series at -2500</a>.</p>
<p>The Suns will host the winner of Wednesday's game between the Los Angeles Clippers and Golden State Warriors. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/clippers-warriors-playin-prediction-04-15-2026/">The Clippers are -208 favorites in the game</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 11:46:17 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285116/Deni-Avdijas-41-Points-Leads-Blazers-Back-To-Playoffs-For-First-Time-Since-2021</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Avdija_Deni_por_241028.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Russell Westbrook Will Return To Kings If Wanted Back</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285115/Russell-Westbrook-Will-Return-To-Kings-If-Wanted-Back</link>
      <description><p>Russell Westbrook's season with the Sacramento Kings ended with the team tied for last place in the Western Conference and Westbrook sidelined due to injury. Despite the downer ending to the year, Westbrook would like to return to Sacramento next season.</p>
<p>"If I'm welcomed back, then I'll be back," Westbrook said when asked if he'd return to the Kings for another season.</p>
<p>The veteran point guard was signed shortly before the start of the regular season. Westbrook went on to average 15.2 points, 5.4 rebounds, 6.7 assists and 1.3 steals in 64 games.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 09:57:31 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285115/Russell-Westbrook-Will-Return-To-Kings-If-Wanted-Back</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Westbrook_Russell_sac_251103.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>2026 NBA Play-In Snapshots (April 14): Hornets/Heat, Blazers/Suns</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285114/2026-NBA-Play-In-Snapshots-April-14-Hornets-Heat-Blazers-Suns</link>
      <description><p>What happened in two snapshots in the NBA Play-In on Tuesday, April 14, 2026.</p>
<p><img style="width: 100%; top: -63px;" src="https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HF8S90RXcAA_Des?format=jpg&name=medium" alt="" data-aria-label-part="" /></p>
<p><img style="width: 100%; top: -63px;" src="https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HF8WGcsW4AAjjvv?format=jpg&name=medium" alt="" data-aria-label-part="" /></p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 09:32:40 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285114/2026-NBA-Play-In-Snapshots-April-14-Hornets-Heat-Blazers-Suns</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Avdija_Deni_por_241028.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Erik Spoelstra Says LaMelo Ball 'Should Have Been Thrown Out' For Tripping Bam Adebayo</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285112/Erik-Spoelstra-Says-LaMelo-Ball-Should-Have-Been-Thrown-Out-For-Tripping-Bam-Adebayo</link>
      <description><p>Head coach Erik Spoelstra said that LaMelo Ball should have been ejected from the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285111/LaMelo-Ball-Scores-30-Points-In-Hornets-Win-Over-Heat-In-Play-In-Game">Miami Heat's 127-126 loss</a> to the Charlotte Hornets in the play-in game after tripping Bam Adebayo in the second quarter.</p>
<p>"I didn't see it [when it happened], but I don't think it's cute," Spoelstra said after the loss. "I don't think it's funny. I think it's a stupid play. It's a dangerous play. Obviously, our best player was out.</p>
<p>"I'm not making an excuse. The Hornets played great and they made those plays down the stretch. We had our opportunities to win. That's a shame. You should be penalized for that. I don't think that belongs in the game -- tripping guys, shenanigans."</p>
<p>Adebayo was ruled out for the game due to a lower back injury. The center had six points and three rebounds when he was injured.</p>
<p>Spoelstra then put blame on the refs for not seeing the trip.</p>
<p>"Somebody has got to see that and that he should have been thrown out of the game for that," added Spoelstra. "I don't know him from anyone. There's no place in the game for that."</p>
<p>Ball, who finished with 30 points and 10 assists, apologized for the trip after the game.</p>
<p>"I apologize on that one," Ball said. "I got hit in the head [on the play] and didn't really know where I was. But I'm going to check on him, see if he's OK and everything."</p>
<p>With the loss, the Heat missed the playoffs for the first time since the 2018-19 season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 01:26:48 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285112/Erik-Spoelstra-Says-LaMelo-Ball-Should-Have-Been-Thrown-Out-For-Tripping-Bam-Adebayo</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Adebayo_Bam_mia_220725.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>LaMelo Ball Scores 30 Points In Hornets' Win Over Heat In Play-In Game</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285111/LaMelo-Ball-Scores-30-Points-In-Hornets-Win-Over-Heat-In-Play-In-Game</link>
      <description><p>The Charlotte Hornets defeated the Miami Heat 127-126 in the play-in game on Tuesday night, eliminating the Heat from the playoffs.</p>
<p>LaMelo Ball had 30 points and 10 assists in the win, with Miles Bridges adding 28 points and nine rebounds.</p>
<p>Ball is now the fourth player to have at least 30 points and 10 assists in a play-in game.</p>
<p>Davion Mitchell finished with 28 points for the Heat. Andrew Wiggins added 27 points in the loss.</p>
<p>Bam Adebayo left the game with a lower back injury in the second quarter after he was tripped by Ball.</p>
<p>The Hornets are set to face the loser of the matchup between the Sixers and Magic on Friday.</p>
<p>The Heat will miss the playoffs for the first time since the 2018-19 season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 01:17:24 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285111/LaMelo-Ball-Scores-30-Points-In-Hornets-Win-Over-Heat-In-Play-In-Game</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Ball_Lamelo_cha_201215.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves Out Indefinitely; Lakers Won't Give Update This Week</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285110/Luka-Doncic-Austin-Reaves-Out-Indefinitely;-Lakers-Wont-Give-Update-This-Week</link>
      <description><p>Los Angeles Lakers coach JJ Redick confirmed Tuesday that Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves remain out indefinitely as the team begins preparation for its first-round playoff series against the Houston Rockets, leaving the club without its starting backcourt for at least the early stages of the postseason.</p>
<p>"They're out indefinitely. I'm not going to have an update for you this week," Redick said, repeating the message for emphasis.</p>
<p>Doncic, who suffered a Grade 2 hamstring strain on April 2, has been in Madrid undergoing treatment at Real Madrid's facilities, where members of his medical team are based. A league source confirmed he is set to fly back to Los Angeles on Friday to rejoin the team ahead of Game 1, though he will not play. Grade 2 hamstring strains typically carry a four-to-six week recovery timeline.</p>
<p>Reaves sustained a Grade 2 oblique strain in the same game and has been receiving treatment in Los Angeles. He attended Tuesday's practice but did not participate. His injury carries an identical four-to-six week timetable.</p>
<p>With both guards sidelined, Redick said the organization's focus is on extending the series long enough for the two to potentially return later in the playoffs. Redick indicated the burden of playmaking will not fall exclusively on 41-year-old LeBron James.</p>
<p>"That's what we've had to do for 82 games for the last two years, so it's not any different," Redick said. "We're mindful. I do think we have other options other than just him having the ball and going against a set defense every single possession."</p>
<p>Redick said the Lakers distilled their series preparation against Houston to two priorities.</p>
<p>"Take care of the ball and box out," he said.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/">The Lakers enter the series as +550 underdogs</a>, according to oddsmakers.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 14 Apr 2026 21:08:15 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285110/Luka-Doncic-Austin-Reaves-Out-Indefinitely;-Lakers-Wont-Give-Update-This-Week</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Doncic_Luka_lal_250327.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>2026 NBA Draft Set For Two Nights Beginning Tuesday June 23</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285109/2026-NBA-Draft-Set-For-Two-Nights-Beginning-Tuesday-June-23</link>
      <description><p>The NBA has scheduled the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/draft_simulator">2026 NBA draft</a> for Tuesday, June 23rd and Wednesday June 24th. Both nights will be televised by ESPN and begin at 8 p.m. ET.</p>
<p>The <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/lottery_simulator">NBA draft lottery</a> is scheduled for Sunday, May 10th. The Washington Wizards, Indiana Pacers and Brooklyn Nets each share a 14 percent chance of landing the number one overall pick and a 52.1 percent chance of a top-4 pick. The Utah Jazz are close behind in terms of the odds at 48.1 percent in the top-4 and 12.5 percent at No. 1. </p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285013/AJ-Dybantsa-Moves-To-No-1-In-ESPNs-2026-NBA-Draft-Big-Board-Top-Of-Class-Remains-Open">A.J. Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson, Cameron Boozer and Caleb Wilson are expected to be the first four picks in some order</a>. There are several highly touted guards right behind them in Keaton Wagler, Darius Acufff Jr., Kingston Flemings and Mikel Brown Jr.</p>
<p>The NBA draft has historically been held on a Thursday, but they switched to a Wednesday and Thursday format when they began to hold the first and second round on separate nights. The NBA is believed to have moved up the 2026 draft to a Tuesday and Wednesday in order to avoid competing against the United States' final World Cup group stage match against T&uuml;rkiye on Thursday, June 25th in primetime. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 14 Apr 2026 16:11:21 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285109/2026-NBA-Draft-Set-For-Two-Nights-Beginning-Tuesday-June-23</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Dybantsa_AJ_byu_251212.jpg" />
   </item>
